svg.title: DevSecOps target logical architecture — pipeline and delivery
svg.desc: Six-layer logical architecture diagram showing source code flowing through GitHub Actions CI with shift-left security gates (SonarCloud, Trivy), artifact registry, Argo CD GitOps sync, GKE Autopilot deployment, Playwright E2E post-sync hooks, and Terraform-managed GCP infrastructure with Workload Identity keyless auth.

g.onclick: sendPrompt('How does Terraform provision the GKE Autopilot cluster?')
sendPrompt('What does the GKE Autopilot cluster provide?')
sendPrompt('What is Artifact Registry used for?')
sendPrompt('How does Workload Identity Federation eliminate static keys?')
sendPrompt('What is stored in the Git repository?')
sendPrompt('What does SonarCloud check in the pipeline?')
sendPrompt('What does Trivy scan in the pipeline?')
sendPrompt('What does Vitest check and why is it a security gate?')

svg.text: shift-left: all security gates run before any image is built

g.onclick: sendPrompt('What triggers the GitHub Actions pipeline?')
sendPrompt('How are Docker images built and tagged in the pipeline?')
sendPrompt('How does the pipeline push images and update manifests?')
sendPrompt('How does Argo CD sync manifests from Git to GKE?')
sendPrompt('What Kubernetes resources does Argo CD deploy?')
sendPrompt('How do Playwright PostSync jobs work in Argo CD?')
sendPrompt('What runs inside the GKE Autopilot namespace?')
sendPrompt('How does Prometheus collect metrics from nginx?')
sendPrompt('How does Wazuh monitor the cluster at runtime?')

svg.text: Zero Trust: default-deny NetworkPolicies + explicit allow rules between all pods
Keyless auth: GitHub Actions -> GCP via OIDC/Workload Identity (no static JSON keys).
GitOps: Argo CD continuously reconciles cluster state from Git (no imperative kubectl).
Shift-left: SonarCloud + Trivy gates run before image build; secrets are blocked early.
